devanagari: श्रीनारद उवाच । भक्तियोगस्य तत्सर्वमन्तरायतयार्भकः । मन्यमानो हृषीकेशं स्मयमान उवाच ह ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīnārada uvācabhaktiyogasya tatsarvamantarāyatayārbhakaḥmanyamāno hṛṣīkeśaṃ smayamāna uvāca ha
translation:
Nārada said:
The child, Prahlāda who looked upon all that (offer of boons etc.) as an impediment to the practice of Bhakti-Yoga (Yoga of Devotion), slightly smiled, and addressed Lord Nṛsiṃha (the Lord of the sense organs) as follows:

devanagari: श्रीप्रह्राद उवाच । मा मां प्रलोभयोत्पत्त्या सक्तंकामेषु तैर्वरैः । तत्सङ्गभीतो निर्विण्णो मुमुक्षुस्त्वामुपाश्रितः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīprahrāda uvācamā māṃ pralobhayotpattyā saktaṃkāmeṣu tairvaraiḥtatsaṅgabhīto nirviṇṇo mumukṣustvāmupāśritaḥ
translation:
Prahlāda said:
Pray, do not tempt me who am addicted by nature (lit. birth) to pleasures, with (the promise of) those boons. Being afraid of contacts with them and in (sheer) disgust of them, I have resorted to you with the desire of securing liberation (from saṃsāra).

devanagari: भृत्यलक्षणजिज्ञासुर्भक्तं कामेष्वचोदयत् । भवान्संसारबीजेषु हृदयग्रन्थिषु प्रभो ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: bhṛtyalakṣaṇajijñāsurbhaktaṃ kāmeṣvacodayatbhavānsaṃsārabījeṣu hṛdayagranthiṣu prabho
translation: Oh Lord! Meseems it is with the desire of ascertaining the characteristics of a real servant or devotee that your worship directed me to choose objects of pleasure which are the seeds of the transmigration of the Self (saṃsāra) and are like so many knots binding the heart.

devanagari: नान्यथा तेऽखिलगुरो घटेत करुणात्मनः । यस्त आशिष आशास्ते न स भृत्यः स वै वणिक् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: nānyathā te'khilaguro ghaṭeta karuṇātmanaḥyasta āśiṣa āśāste na sa bhṛtyaḥ sa vai vaṇik
translation: Otherwise that (offer) would have been impossible from you who are full of mercy, Oh Father of all![1] He who hankers after blessings from you, is but a merchant, and not a real servant (devotee)[2].
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Some editions add here:
naṣṭa-dṛṣṭes tamasyāndhe tvamekaḥ pāradarśanaḥ /
You are the only guide to lead across to the other shore of the ocean of saṃsāra, the people who have lost their eye-sight in blinding darkness.

Footnote [2]:
After this line Andhra edition adds:
sa vai svāmī, sa vai bhṛtyo, guṇalubdhau na kāmukau!
Verily he is the master and he is the servant. They are so due to their appreciation of qualities, but not for any gain or pleasure.

devanagari: आशासानो न वै भृत्यः स्वामिन्याशिष आत्मनः । न स्वामी भृत्यतः स्वाम्यमिच्छन्यो राति चाशिषः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: āśāsāno na vai bhṛtyaḥ svāminyāśiṣa ātmanaḥna svāmī bhṛtyataḥ svāmyamicchanyo rāti cāśiṣaḥ
translation: Verily he is not a real servant who desires to secure worldly pleasures (in return for services) from his master. Nor is he a real master who grants such comforts to play the lord upon his servant.

devanagari: अहं त्वकामस्त्वद्भक्तस्त्वं च स्वाम्यनपाश्रयः । नान्यथेहावयोरर्थो राजसेवकयोरिव ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: ahaṃ tvakāmastvadbhaktastvaṃ ca svāmyanapāśrayaḥnānyathehāvayorartho rājasevakayoriva
translation: But I am your devotee who cherishes no desire and you are the Master who expect nothing (from your servant). Here, between us, there is no object (to be gained) as between the king and his servant.

devanagari: यदि दास्यसि मे कामान्वरांस्त्वं वरदर्षभ । कामानां हृद्यसंरोहं भवतस्तु वृणे वरम् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: yadi dāsyasi me kāmānvarāṃstvaṃ varadarṣabhakāmānāṃ hṛdyasaṃrohaṃ bhavatastu vṛṇe varam
translation: Oh foremost one among the bestowers of boons! If (at all) You (want to) confer any boons on me, Oh Lord, I seek of your Worship the boon that no desire should germinate in my heart.

devanagari: इन्द्रि याणि मनः प्राण आत्मा धर्मो धृतिर्मतिः । ह्रीः श्रीस्तेजः स्मृतिः सत्यं यस्य नश्यन्ति जन्मना ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: indri yāṇi manaḥ prāṇa ātmā dharmo dhṛtirmatiḥhrīḥ śrīstejaḥ smṛtiḥ satyaṃ yasya naśyanti janmanā
translation: For, with the rise of desires, the (powers of) the senses, the mind, the vital air, the Soul, righteousness, fortitude, judgment, modesty, prosperity, energy, memory and truth come to an end (once for all).

devanagari: विमुञ्चति यदा कामान्मानवो मनसि स्थितान् । तर्ह्येव पुण्डरीकाक्ष भगवत्त्वाय कल्पते ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: vimuñcati yadā kāmānmānavo manasi sthitāntarhyeva puṇḍarīkākṣa bhagavattvāya kalpate
translation: [3] When a man gets rid of desires fixed (deep) in the heart, then alone he becomes fit to attain to thy status, Oh lotus-eyed Lord.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Here Bhāvāratha Dīpikā quotes Kaṭha Upa. 2.3. 14—
yadā sarve pramucyante kāmā ye'sya hṛdi śritāḥ /
atha martyomṛto bhavatyatra brahma samaśnute //
When one gets rid of all the desires residing (fixed) in one’s heart, the mortal becomes immortal immediately and attains oneness with Brahman in this very life.

devanagari: ॐ नमो भगवते तुभ्यं पुरुषाय महात्मने । हरयेऽद्भुतसिंहाय ब्रह्मणे परमात्मने ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: oṃ namo bhagavate tubhyaṃ puruṣāya mahātmaneharaye'dbhutasiṃhāya brahmaṇe paramātmane
translation:
(Now I remember the Mantra to which I was initiated by Nārada).
Salutations to You, Oh Almighty Lord, the Supreme Person, the High-Souled One, Hari, the wonderful lion. Supreme Brahman and Paramātinan.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । नैकान्तिनो मे मयि जात्विहाशिष आशासतेऽमुत्र च ये भवद्विधाः । तथापि मन्वन्तरमेतदत्र दैत्येश्वराणामनुभुङ्क्ष्व भोगान् ॥ ११...
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvācanaikāntino me mayi jātvihāśiṣa āśāsate'mutra ca ye bhavadvidhāḥtathāpi manvantarametadatra daityeśvarāṇāmanubhuṅkṣva bhogān
translation:
Lord Nṛsiṃha said:
Those who are exclusively devoted to Me like you, never expect from Me any blessing in this or in the next world Still (it is my order that you shall enjoy throughout this Manvantara the luxuries of the overlordship of the Daityas here.

devanagari: कथा मदीया जुषमाणः प्रियास्त्वमावेश्य मामात्मनि सन्तमेकम् । सर्वेषु भूतेष्वधियज्ञमीशं यजस्व योगेन च कर्म हिन्वन् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: kathā madīyā juṣamāṇaḥ priyāstvamāveśya māmātmani santamekamsarveṣu bhūteṣvadhiyajñamīśaṃ yajasva yogena ca karma hinvan
translation: You continue to enjoy listening to my stories so dear to you. Realize within your own heart Me, the Lord present in all beings, and the presiding deity of sacrifices. Dedicating all your activities to Me, worship Me, in meditation.

devanagari: भोगेन पुण्यं कुशलेन पापं कलेवरं कालजवेन हित्वा । कीर्तिं विशुद्धां सुरलोकगीतां विताय मामेष्यसि मुक्तबन्धः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: bhogena puṇyaṃ kuśalena pāpaṃ kalevaraṃ kālajavena hitvākīrtiṃ viśuddhāṃ suralokagītāṃ vitāya māmeṣyasi muktabandhaḥ
translation: Having exhausted your merit by enjoying pleasure and sin by doing meritorious actions (or by prāyaścitta—expiatory rites) and given up your body in course of time, and spread such extremely pure renown as to be sung in heaven, you shall attain to Me completely liberated from all bonds.

devanagari: य एतत्कीर्तयेन्मह्यं त्वया गीतमिदं नरः । त्वां च मां च स्मरन्काले कर्मबन्धात्प्रमुच्यते ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: ya etatkīrtayenmahyaṃ tvayā gītamidaṃ naraḥtvāṃ ca māṃ ca smarankāle karmabandhātpramucyate
translation: A person who recites this song (prayer) sung by you to Me and remembers you and Me, becomes free from the bondage of karman in due course.

devanagari: श्रीप्रह्राद उवाच । वरं वरय एतत्ते वरदेशान्महेश्वर । यदनिन्दत्पिता मे त्वामविद्वांस्तेज ऐश्वरम् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīprahrāda uvācavaraṃ varaya etatte varadeśānmaheśvarayadanindatpitā me tvāmavidvāṃsteja aiśvaram
translation:
Prahlāda said:
Oh Supreme Lord! The Lord of those who graciously confer boons! I seek from you this boon that my father, ignorant of Your being the Divine Light, and with his mind overwhelmed (lit. pierced through) with indignation, reviled you who are the veritable Father of the World and the Master, by calling You the murderer of his brother, and through wrong outlook, behaved sinfully to me, Your devotee. May (my father) be absolved of the terrible sins which are too difficult to be crossed, even though he must have been purified when he was glanced at by you who are compassionate to the afflicted.

devanagari: विद्धामर्षाशयः साक्षात्सर्वलोकगुरुं प्रभुम् । भ्रातृहेति मृषादृष्टिस्त्वद्भक्ते मयि चाघवान् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: viddhāmarṣāśayaḥ sākṣātsarvalokaguruṃ prabhumbhrātṛheti mṛṣādṛṣṭistvadbhakte mayi cāghavān
translation:
Prahlāda said:
Oh Supreme Lord! The Lord of those who graciously confer boons! I seek from you this boon that my father, ignorant of Your being the Divine Light, and with his mind overwhelmed (lit. pierced through) with indignation, reviled you who are the veritable Father of the World and the Master, by calling You the murderer of his brother, and through wrong outlook, behaved sinfully to me, Your devotee. May (my father) be absolved of the terrible sins which are too difficult to be crossed, even though he must have been purified when he was glanced at by you who are compassionate to the afflicted.

devanagari: तस्मात्पिता मे पूयेत दुरन्ताद्दुस्तरादघात् । पूतस्तेऽपाङ्गसंदृष्टस्तदा कृपणवत्सल ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmātpitā me pūyeta durantāddustarādaghātpūtaste'pāṅgasaṃdṛṣṭastadā kṛpaṇavatsala
translation:
Prahlāda said:
Oh Supreme Lord! The Lord of those who graciously confer boons! I seek from you this boon that my father, ignorant of Your being the Divine Light, and with his mind overwhelmed (lit. pierced through) with indignation, reviled you who are the veritable Father of the World and the Master, by calling You the murderer of his brother, and through wrong outlook, behaved sinfully to me, Your devotee. May (my father) be absolved of the terrible sins which are too difficult to be crossed, even though he must have been purified when he was glanced at by you who are compassionate to the afflicted.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । त्रिःसप्तभिः पिता पूतः पितृभिः सह तेऽनघ । यत्साधोऽस्य कुले जातो भवान्वै कुलपावनः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvācatriḥsaptabhiḥ pitā pūtaḥ pitṛbhiḥ saha te'naghayatsādho'sya kule jāto bhavānvai kulapāvanaḥ
translation:
The Lord said:
Oh sinless righteous saint! Inasmuch as you, the purifier of the family, have been born in his house, your father along with twenty-one generations of his fore-fathers stands absolved of all sins[4].
purport:
Footnote [4]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā explains that as Hiraṇyakaśipu was the 3rd in generation from god Brahmā, here the twenty-one generations of his previous birth are implied.
VC. adds: Twenty-one generations of forefathers in your (Prahlāda’s) twenty-one previous incarnations are included here.

devanagari: यत्र यत्र च मद्भक्ताः प्रशान्ताः समदर्शिनः । साधवः समुदाचारास्ते पूयन्तेऽपि कीकटाः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: yatra yatra ca madbhaktāḥ praśāntāḥ samadarśinaḥsādhavaḥ samudācārāste pūyante'pi kīkaṭāḥ
translation: Wherever my devotees, extremely serene, impartial in outlook, noble in character and of pious behaviour, are present, they sanctify that country (along with its inhabitants) even if it be sinful like that of Kīkaṭa.

devanagari: सर्वात्मना न हिंसन्ति भूतग्रामेषु किञ्चन । उच्चावचेषु दैत्येन्द्र मद्भावविगतस्पृहाः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: sarvātmanā na hiṃsanti bhūtagrāmeṣu kiñcanauccāvaceṣu daityendra madbhāvavigataspṛhāḥ
translation: Oh Lord of Daityas! They (My devotees) who, out of devotion unto Me, have become devoid of desires[5] (which are at the root of anger, violence etc.), do not trouble any creature in any category of beings, high or low, by any means.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
mad-bhāvena gataspṛhaḥ—Realizing my presence everywhere, they entertain no jealousy against anyone—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: भवन्ति पुरुषा लोके मद्भक्तास्त्वामनुव्रताः । भवान्मे खलु भक्तानां सर्वेषां प्रतिरूपधृक् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: bhavanti puruṣā loke madbhaktāstvāmanuvratāḥbhavānme khalu bhaktānāṃ sarveṣāṃ pratirūpadhṛk
translation: In this world, there are men who follow your way of life and become My devotees. But certainly, you stand as a model to all my devotees.

devanagari: कुरु त्वं प्रेतकृत्यानि पितुः पूतस्य सर्वशः । मदङ्गस्पर्शनेनाङ्ग लोकान्यास्यति सुप्रजाः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: kuru tvaṃ pretakṛtyāni pituḥ pūtasya sarvaśaḥmadaṅgasparśanenāṅga lokānyāsyati suprajāḥ
translation: Oh child Prahlāda! (As a formality) perform the obsequies of your father who has been thoroughly purified through contact with My person (and has become hence sinless). Now, being blest with you as a pious son, he will certainly attain to higher worlds.

devanagari: पित्र्यं च स्थानमातिष्ठ यथोक्तं ब्रह्मवादिभिः । मय्यावेश्य मनस्तात कुरु कर्माणि मत्परः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: pitryaṃ ca sthānamātiṣṭha yathoktaṃ brahmavādibhiḥmayyāveśya manastāta kuru karmāṇi matparaḥ
translation: As directed by exponents of the Vedas, you occupy the throne of your father. Concentrating your mind on Me, carry out your duties with complete devotion to Me, Oh child!

devanagari: श्रीनारद उवाच । प्रह्रादोऽपि तथा चक्रे पितुर्यत्साम्परायिकम् । यथाह भगवान्राजन्नभिषिक्तो द्विजातिभिः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīnārada uvācaprahrādo'pi tathā cakre pituryatsāmparāyikamyathāha bhagavānrājannabhiṣikto dvijātibhiḥ
translation:
Nārada said:
In compliance of the command of the Lord, Prahlāda also performed all the funeral rites of his father. And he was installed as a king by excellent Brāhmaṇas (and governed as per instructions of the Lord.)

devanagari: प्रसादसुमुखं दृष्ट्वा ब्रह्मा नरहरिं हरिम् । स्तुत्वा वाग्भिः पवित्राभिः प्राह देवादिभिर्वृतः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: prasādasumukhaṃ dṛṣṭvā brahmā narahariṃ harimstutvā vāgbhiḥ pavitrābhiḥ prāha devādibhirvṛtaḥ
translation: Seeing the countenance of the Man-Lion Hari graciously pleased, Brahmā who was surrounded with gods, praised Him with holy Vedic hymns and submitted (as follows):

devanagari: श्रीब्रह्मोवाच । देवदेवाखिलाध्यक्ष भूतभावन पूर्वज । दिष्ट्या ते निहतः पापो लोकसन्तापनोऽसुरः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībrahmovācadevadevākhilādhyakṣa bhūtabhāvana pūrvajadiṣṭyā te nihataḥ pāpo lokasantāpano'suraḥ
translation:
Brahmā said:
Oh God of gods! The Supreme Ruler of all! The Creator of all beings! Oh the First born one! It is fortunate that You have slain the wicked demon, the tormentor of the world.

devanagari: योऽसौ लब्धवरो मत्तो न वध्यो मम सृष्टिभिः । तपोयोगबलोन्नद्धः समस्तनिगमानहन् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: yo'sau labdhavaro matto na vadhyo mama sṛṣṭibhiḥtapoyogabalonnaddhaḥ samastanigamānahan
translation: It was this demon who, having secured boons from me, could not be killed by anyone of my creation. Being arrogant with (the powers acquired by) penance and Yoga and with prowess he violated the code of righteousness.

devanagari: दिष्ट्या तत्तनयः साधुर्महाभागवतोऽर्भकः । त्वया विमोचितो मृत्योर्दिष्ट्या त्वां समितोऽधुना ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: diṣṭyā tattanayaḥ sādhurmahābhāgavato'rbhakaḥtvayā vimocito mṛtyordiṣṭyā tvāṃ samito'dhunā
translation: Luckily, his son, though a child, is a pious Soul and a great devotee of Yours. Fortunately he has been saved by You from death, and he has now attained to You.

devanagari: एतद्वपुस्ते भगवन्ध्यायतः परमात्मनः । सर्वतो गोप्तृ सन्त्रासान्मृत्योरपि जिघांसतः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: etadvapuste bhagavandhyāyataḥ paramātmanaḥsarvato goptṛ santrāsānmṛtyorapi jighāṃsataḥ
translation: Oh Lord! He who, with concentrated mind, contemplates on this form of Yours, will be protected on all sides from fear—even from the god of death wishing to kill one.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । मैवं विभोऽसुराणां ते प्रदेयः पद्मसम्भव । वरः क्रूरनिसर्गाणामहीनाममृतं यथा ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvācamaivaṃ vibho'surāṇāṃ te pradeyaḥ padmasambhavavaraḥ krūranisargāṇāmahīnāmamṛtaṃ yathā
translation:
Nṛsiṃha said:
Oh lotus-born god! No such boon is to be conferred by you on Asuras. A boon bestowed on those who are cruel by nature, is harmful like feeding serpents with milk.

devanagari: श्रीनारद उवाच । इत्युक्त्वा भगवान्राजंस्ततश्चान्तर्दधे हरिः । अदृश्यः सर्वभूतानां पूजितः परमेष्ठिना ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīnārada uvācaityuktvā bhagavānrājaṃstataścāntardadhe hariḥadṛśyaḥ sarvabhūtānāṃ pūjitaḥ parameṣṭhinā
translation:
Nārada said:
Oh king Yudhiṣṭhira! Having spoken thus, Lord Hari Who is normally invisible to all beings and Who was worshipped by god Brahmā, vanished on that very spot.

devanagari: ततः सम्पूज्य शिरसा ववन्दे परमेष्ठिनम् । भवं प्रजापतीन्देवान्प्रह्रादो भगवत्कलाः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ sampūjya śirasā vavande parameṣṭhinambhavaṃ prajāpatīndevānprahrādo bhagavatkalāḥ
translation: Then Prahlāda worshipped god Brahmā, Śiva, Prajāpatis and gods—all of whom were parts of the Almighty Lord—and bowed them with his head.

devanagari: ततः काव्यादिभिः सार्धं मुनिभिः कमलासनः । दैत्यानां दानवानां च प्रह्रादमकरोत्पतिम् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ kāvyādibhiḥ sārdhaṃ munibhiḥ kamalāsanaḥdaityānāṃ dānavānāṃ ca prahrādamakarotpatim
translation: Thereafter, the lotus-seated god Brahmā, together with sages headed by Śukra, crowned Prahlāda as the Lord of Daityas and Dānavas.

devanagari: प्रतिनन्द्य ततो देवाः प्रयुज्य परमाशिषः । स्वधामानि ययू राजन्ब्रह्माद्याः प्रतिपूजिताः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: pratinandya tato devāḥ prayujya paramāśiṣaḥsvadhāmāni yayū rājanbrahmādyāḥ pratipūjitāḥ
translation: Then, having felicitated Prahlāda and bestowed their highest blessings on him, the gods headed by Brahmā who were worshipped by him again, returned to their respective places, Oh King.

devanagari: एवं च पार्षदौ विष्णोः पुत्रत्वं प्रापितौ दितेः । हृदि स्थितेन हरिणा वैरभावेन तौ हतौ ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ ca pārṣadau viṣṇoḥ putratvaṃ prāpitau diteḥhṛdi sthitena hariṇā vairabhāvena tau hatau
translation: In this way, the two attendants of Viṣṇu (Jaya and Vijaya) who were born as sons to Diti (due to the imprecation of Brāhmaṇas) were killed by Hari, present in their heart due to the hostile attitude adopted by them towards Hari.

devanagari: पुनश्च विप्रशापेन राक्षसौ तौ बभूवतुः । कुम्भकर्णदशग्रीवौ हतौ तौ रामविक्रमैः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: punaśca vipraśāpena rākṣasau tau babhūvatuḥkumbhakarṇadaśagrīvau hatau tau rāmavikramaiḥ
translation: And due to the curse of a Brāhmaṇa, both of them were born again as the demons Kumbhakarṇa and Rāvaṇa of ten heads, who were killed by Rāma’s arrows.

devanagari: शयानौ युधि निर्भिन्न हृदयौ रामशायकैः । तच्चित्तौ जहतुर्देहं यथा प्राक्तनजन्मनि ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: śayānau yudhi nirbhinna hṛdayau rāmaśāyakaiḥtaccittau jahaturdehaṃ yathā prāktanajanmani
translation: While lying on the field of battle with their hearts pierced with Rāma’s arrows, both of them quitted their physical bodies with their hearts fixed on Him (Rāma), as (it happened) in their previous birth (as Hiraṇyākṣa and Hiraṇyakaśipu).

devanagari: ताविहाथ पुनर्जातौ शिशुपालकरूषजौ । हरौ वैरानुबन्धेन पश्यतस्ते समीयतुः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: tāvihātha punarjātau śiśupālakarūṣajauharau vairānubandhena paśyataste samīyatuḥ
translation: Both of those (attendants of Viṣṇu) were now born here as Śiśupāla and Dantavakra. It is in Your very presence that they merged in Lord Hari by entertaining continuous enmity towards Him.

devanagari: एनः पूर्वकृतं यत्तद्राजानः कृष्णवैरिणः । जहुस्तेऽन्ते तदात्मानः कीटः पेशस्कृतो यथा ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: enaḥ pūrvakṛtaṃ yattadrājānaḥ kṛṣṇavairiṇaḥjahuste'nte tadātmānaḥ kīṭaḥ peśaskṛto yathā
translation: Kings who were the enemies of Kṛṣṇa shed off, at the end of their life, whatever sins they have committed in the previous life, and become like unto Him through constant meditation like a caterpillar which, through constant brooding, is transformed into a wasp.

devanagari: यथा यथा भगवतो भक्त्या परमयाभिदा । नृपाश्चैद्यादयः सात्म्यं हरेस्तच्चिन्तया ययुः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: yathā yathā bhagavato bhaktyā paramayābhidānṛpāścaidyādayaḥ sātmyaṃ harestaccintayā yayuḥ
translation: Just as votaries of the Lord, through their unflinching devotion to Hari attain to perfect identity with Him, (inimical) kings like Śiśupāla and others, through their constant brooding over Him (as their enemy) attained to similarity of form like Him.

devanagari: आख्यातं सर्वमेतत्ते यन्मां त्वं परिपृष्टवान् । दमघोषसुतादीनां हरेः सात्म्यमपि द्विषाम् ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: ākhyātaṃ sarvametatte yanmāṃ tvaṃ paripṛṣṭavāndamaghoṣasutādīnāṃ hareḥ sātmyamapi dviṣām
translation: I have answered to you in details all the queries made by you and also the identity with Hari attained by His enemies like the son of Damaghoṣa (Śiśupāla).

devanagari: एषा ब्रह्मण्यदेवस्य कृष्णस्य च महात्मनः । अवतारकथा पुण्या वधो यत्रादिदैत्ययोः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: eṣā brahmaṇyadevasya kṛṣṇasya ca mahātmanaḥavatārakathā puṇyā vadho yatrādidaityayoḥ
translation: This is the sacred story of the (Man-lion) incarnation of the noble-souled Kṛṣṇa, the devotee of Brāhmaṇas—a story (containing the narration) of slaying the two ancient Daityas (viz. Hiraṇyākṣa and Hiraṇyakaśipu).

devanagari: प्रह्रादस्यानुचरितं महाभागवतस्य च । भक्तिर्ज्ञानं विरक्तिश्च याथार्थ्यं चास्य वै हरेः ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: prahrādasyānucaritaṃ mahābhāgavatasya cabhaktirjñānaṃ viraktiśca yāthārthyaṃ cāsya vai hareḥ
translation: This narration includes the life (and character) of Prahlāda, the great votary of the Lord, and (a detailed description of) his devotion, knowledge non-attachment and also of the true nature and an account of the glorious deeds of Hari who is the Lord of the creation, maintenance and the destruction (of the universe). (It describes) the great revolutions in the positions of gods and demons in course of time.

devanagari: सर्गस्थित्यप्ययेशस्य गुणकर्मानुवर्णनम् । परावरेषां स्थानानां कालेन व्यत्ययो महान् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: sargasthityapyayeśasya guṇakarmānuvarṇanamparāvareṣāṃ sthānānāṃ kālena vyatyayo mahān
translation: This narration includes the life (and character) of Prahlāda, the great votary of the Lord, and (a detailed description of) his devotion, knowledge non-attachment and also of the true nature and an account of the glorious deeds of Hari who is the Lord of the creation, maintenance and the destruction (of the universe). (It describes) the great revolutions in the positions of gods and demons in course of time.

devanagari: धर्मो भागवतानां च भगवान्येन गम्यते । आख्यानेऽस्मिन्समाम्नातमाध्यात्मिकमशेषतः ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: dharmo bhāgavatānāṃ ca bhagavānyena gamyateākhyāne'sminsamāmnātamādhyātmikamaśeṣataḥ
translation: In this chronicle has been described the righteous path of the devotees of the Lord which leads to the attainment of (one-ness with) Him; and all topics relating to the soul (spiritual knowledge) have been thoroughly discussed.

devanagari: य एतत्पुण्यमाख्यानं विष्णोर्वीर्योपबृंहितम् । कीर्तयेच्छ्रद्धया श्रुत्वा कर्मपाशैर्विमुच्यते ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: ya etatpuṇyamākhyānaṃ viṣṇorvīryopabṛṃhitamkīrtayecchraddhayā śrutvā karmapāśairvimucyate
translation: Whoever, after reverentially listening to this holy narrative of the heroic deeds of Viṣṇu, recites it to others is liberated from the bonds of Karmas (good or bad).

devanagari: एतद्य आदिपुरुषस्य मृगेन्द्र लीलां । दैत्येन्द्र यूथपवधं प्रयतः पठेत । दैत्यात्मजस्य च सतां प्रवरस्य पुण्यं । श्रुत्वानुभावमकुतोभयमेति ल...
english_devnagari: etadya ādipuruṣasya mṛgendra līlāṃdaityendra yūthapavadhaṃ prayataḥ paṭhetadaityātmajasya ca satāṃ pravarasya puṇyaṃśrutvānubhāvamakutobhayameti lokam
translation: Whoever peruses piously with concentrated mind the sportive actions of Lord Viṣṇu (the Most Ancient Person) in assuming the Lion form and slaying Hiraṇyakaśipu, the leader of the legions of demons, and listens to (lit. having listened to) the (recital of the) hallowing glory of the demon Hirayṇyakaśipu’s son (Prahlāda), the foremost among the righteous, attains to the region (Mokṣa) which is beyond the range of fear.

devanagari: यूयं नृलोके बत भूरिभागा लोकं पुनाना मुनयोऽभियन्ति । येषां गृहानावसतीति साक्षाद्गूढं परं ब्रह्म मनुष्यलिङ्गम् ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: yūyaṃ nṛloke bata bhūribhāgā lokaṃ punānā munayo'bhiyantiyeṣāṃ gṛhānāvasatīti sākṣādgūḍhaṃ paraṃ brahma manuṣyaliṅgam
translation: (Oh Yudhiṣṭhira!) You are certainly immensely more fortunate in this world (as compared with Prahlāda etc. his spiritual preceptor Nārada, as well as your forefathers and other great sages) inasmuch as great sages who sanctify the world, pay visit to you from all quarters, and the Supreme Brahman Himself, concealing His greatness in the guise of a human being, resides with you in your houses.

devanagari: स वा अयं ब्रह्म महद्विमृग्य कैवल्यनिर्वाणसुखानुभूतिः । प्रियः सुहृद्वः खलु मातुलेय आत्मार्हणीयो विधिकृद्गुरुश्च ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: sa vā ayaṃ brahma mahadvimṛgya kaivalyanirvāṇasukhānubhūtiḥpriyaḥ suhṛdvaḥ khalu mātuleya ātmārhaṇīyo vidhikṛdguruśca
translation: This Kṛṣṇa who is your dear friend and well-wisher, your maternal uncle’s son, your very adorable self, guide and advisor carrying out your behests (as your representative or charioteer), is verily the Brahman who is the embodiment of the ecstatic experience of the absolute Supreme bliss (unconditioned by any upādhi) which is sought after by the great.

devanagari: न यस्य साक्षाद्भवपद्मजादिभी रूपं धिया वस्तुतयोपवर्णितम् । मौनेन भक्त्योपशमेन पूजितः प्रसीदतामेष स सात्वतां पतिः ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: na yasya sākṣādbhavapadmajādibhī rūpaṃ dhiyā vastutayopavarṇitammaunena bhaktyopaśamena pūjitaḥ prasīdatāmeṣa sa sātvatāṃ patiḥ
translation: May Lord Kṛṣṇa, the Lord of the Sātvatas whose direct essential nature has not been actually comprehended or described with their intellect by gods like Śiva and Brahmā, and who has been devoutly worshipped by us through silent meditation añd renunciation, be gracious unto us. [The Supreme Brahman does not reside in Prahlāda’s palace. Nor do sages go there to have an audience with him. Nor does He act like a cousin. Hence, Oh Yudhiṣṭhira and Pāṇḍavas, you are immensely more fortunate than Prahlāda and we.]

devanagari: स एष भगवान्राजन्व्यतनोद्विहतं यशः । पुरा रुद्र स्य देवस्य मयेनानन्तमायिना ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: sa eṣa bhagavānrājanvyatanodvihataṃ yaśaḥpurā rudra sya devasya mayenānantamāyinā
translation: Oh Yudhiṣṭhira! he is that very Lord who, in days of yore, extended the glory of god Rudra as it was eclipsed by Maya, the master of innumerable delusive powers.

devanagari: राजोवाच । कस्मिन्कर्मणि देवस्य मयोऽहन्जगदीशितुः । यथा चोपचिता कीर्तिः कृष्णेनानेन कथ्यताम् ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: rājovācakasminkarmaṇi devasya mayo'hanjagadīśituḥyathā copacitā kīrtiḥ kṛṣṇenānena kathyatām
translation:
The King (Yudhiṣṭhira) asked:
Be pleased to describe to us in which action Maya destroyed the glory of god Śiva and the way in which Lord Kṛṣṇa (re-established and) enhanced it.

devanagari: श्रीनारद उवाच । निर्जिता असुरा देवैर्युध्यनेनोपबृंहितैः । मायिनां परमाचार्यं मयं शरणमाययुः ॥ ५३ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīnārada uvācanirjitā asurā devairyudhyanenopabṛṃhitaiḥmāyināṃ paramācāryaṃ mayaṃ śaraṇamāyayuḥ
translation:
Nārada said:
Asuras who were completely vanquished in the battle by gods who were strengthened by Lord Kṛṣṇa, sought the refuge of Maya the Supreme Preceptor of all conjurors (experts in the arts of illusion or magic).

devanagari: स निर्माय पुरस्तिस्रो हैमीरौप्यायसीर्विभुः । दुर्लक्ष्यापायसंयोगा दुर्वितर्क्यपरिच्छदाः ॥ ५४ ॥
english_devnagari: sa nirmāya purastisro haimīraupyāyasīrvibhuḥdurlakṣyāpāyasaṃyogā durvitarkyaparicchadāḥ
translation: The master-conjuror constructed three (flying-fortress-like) cities made of gold, silver and iron respectively. Their movements (arrival and departure) were undetectable and the (war) materials and equipments provided therein were beyond the conception of gods (And he handed these over to Asuras).

devanagari: ताभिस्तेऽसुरसेनान्यो लोकांस्त्रीन्सेश्वरान्नृप । स्मरन्तो नाशयां चक्रुः पूर्ववैरमलक्षिताः ॥ ५५ ॥
english_devnagari: tābhiste'surasenānyo lokāṃstrīnseśvarānnṛpasmaranto nāśayāṃ cakruḥ pūrvavairamalakṣitāḥ
translation: Remembering their old hostility, the leaders of Asura armies destroyed the three worlds along with the rulers, themselves remaining invisible with the help of those (flying) cities, Oh King.

devanagari: ततस्ते सेश्वरा लोका उपासाद्येश्वरं नताः । त्राहि नस्तावकान्देव विनष्टांस्त्रिपुरालयैः ॥ ५६ ॥
english_devnagari: tataste seśvarā lokā upāsādyeśvaraṃ natāḥtrāhi nastāvakāndeva vinaṣṭāṃstripurālayaiḥ
translation: Oh King, thereupon all residents of the three worlds, along with their rulers, approached god Śiva and prayed, “Be pleased, Oh God, to protect us, Your own people (who are practically) destroyed by Asuras residing in three (flying) cities.

devanagari: अथानुगृह्य भगवान्मा भैष्टेति सुरान्विभुः । शरं धनुषि सन्धाय पुरेष्वस्त्रं व्यमुञ्चत ॥ ५७ ॥
english_devnagari: athānugṛhya bhagavānmā bhaiṣṭeti surānvibhuḥśaraṃ dhanuṣi sandhāya pureṣvastraṃ vyamuñcata
translation: Out of grace unto the celestials, the almighty god Śiva reassured the gods not to be afraid, and fitted an arrow (charged with mystic powers) to his bow, and discharged the missile at the (flying) cities.

devanagari: ततोऽग्निवर्णा इषव उत्पेतुः सूर्यमण्डलात् । यथा मयूखसन्दोहा नादृश्यन्त पुरो यतः ॥ ५८ ॥
english_devnagari: tato'gnivarṇā iṣava utpetuḥ sūryamaṇḍalātyathā mayūkhasandohā nādṛśyanta puro yataḥ
translation: Fiery arrows, like streams of rays emanating from the orb of the Sun, shot forth from that shaft and the cities, being screened by them, became invisible.

devanagari: तैः स्पृष्टा व्यसवः सर्वे निपेतुः स्म पुरौकसः । तानानीय महायोगी मयः कूपरसेऽक्षिपत् ॥ ५९ ॥
english_devnagari: taiḥ spṛṣṭā vyasavaḥ sarve nipetuḥ sma puraukasaḥtānānīya mahāyogī mayaḥ kūparase'kṣipat
translation: Struck with those shafts, all the citizens of those cities, fell dead. But the great Yogī Maya brought them and put them into a well of nectar (excavated by him in the cities).

devanagari: सिद्धामृतरसस्पृष्टा वज्रसारा महौजसः । उत्तस्थुर्मेघदलना वैद्युता इव वह्नयः ॥ ६० ॥
english_devnagari: siddhāmṛtarasaspṛṣṭā vajrasārā mahaujasaḥuttasthurmeghadalanā vaidyutā iva vahnayaḥ
translation: At the touch of the resuscitating fluid prepared by him (Maya). they sprang to life with adamantine bodily frames endowed with extraordinary vigour, like fires of the lightening tearing open the clouds.

devanagari: विलोक्य भग्नसङ्कल्पं विमनस्कं वृषध्वजम् । तदायं भगवान्विष्णुस्तत्रोपायमकल्पयत् ॥ ६१ ॥
english_devnagari: vilokya bhagnasaṅkalpaṃ vimanaskaṃ vṛṣadhvajamtadāyaṃ bhagavānviṣṇustatropāyamakalpayat
translation: Finding god Śiva (the bull-bannered god) frustrated in his undertaking and dejected, this Lord Viṣṇu devised an expendient [expedient?] for it.

devanagari: वत्सश्चासीत्तदा ब्रह्मा स्वयं विष्णुरयं हि गौः । प्रविश्य त्रिपुरं काले रसकूपामृतं पपौ ॥ ६२ ॥
english_devnagari: vatsaścāsīttadā brahmā svayaṃ viṣṇurayaṃ hi gauḥpraviśya tripuraṃ kāle rasakūpāmṛtaṃ papau
translation: This Lord Viṣṇu assumed the form of a cow and Brahmā became the calf. Entering the cities at midday, he drank out the nectar from the well of that (immortalizing) fluid.

devanagari: तेऽसुरा ह्यपि पश्यन्तो न न्यषेधन्विमोहिताः । तद्विज्ञाय महायोगी रसपालानिदं जगौ ॥ ६३ ॥
english_devnagari: te'surā hyapi paśyanto na nyaṣedhanvimohitāḥtadvijñāya mahāyogī rasapālānidaṃ jagau
translation: Though witnessing it, the Asuras (guarding the well) became too bewildered to prevent it. Knowing that and remembering that it was destined to be so, Maya, the past-master in devising expedients addressed the following (words) to the guards of that well of nectar who were stricken with grief: “No one whether a god, a demon, a man or any other being is capable of thwarting what has been ordained by Fate regarding one’s own Self or another or of both.”

devanagari: स्मयन्विशोकः शोकार्तान्स्मरन्दैवगतिं च ताम् । देवोऽसुरो नरोऽन्यो वा नेश्वरोऽस्तीह कश्चन ॥ ६४ ॥
english_devnagari: smayanviśokaḥ śokārtānsmarandaivagatiṃ ca tāmdevo'suro naro'nyo vā neśvaro'stīha kaścana
translation: Though witnessing it, the Asuras (guarding the well) became too bewildered to prevent it. Knowing that and remembering that it was destined to be so, Maya, the past-master in devising expedients addressed the following (words) to the guards of that well of nectar who were stricken with grief: “No one whether a god, a demon, a man or any other being is capable of thwarting what has been ordained by Fate regarding one’s own Self or another or of both.”

devanagari: आत्मनोऽन्यस्य वा दिष्टं दैवेनापोहितुं द्वयोः । अथासौ शक्तिभिः स्वाभिः शम्भोः प्राधानिकं व्यधात् ॥ ६५ ॥
english_devnagari: ātmano'nyasya vā diṣṭaṃ daivenāpohituṃ dvayoḥathāsau śaktibhiḥ svābhiḥ śambhoḥ prādhānikaṃ vyadhāt
translation:
Though witnessing it, the Asuras (guarding the well) became too bewildered to prevent it. Knowing that and remembering that it was destined to be so, Maya, the past-master in devising expedients addressed the following (words) to the guards of that well of nectar who were stricken with grief: “No one whether a god, a demon, a man or any other being is capable of thwarting what has been ordained by Fate regarding one’s own Self or another or of both.”
Then by His own potencies, He (Lord Kṛṣṇa) devised the equipment of battle (war-materials) for god Śiva.

devanagari: धर्मज्ञानविरक्त्यृद्धि तपोविद्याक्रियादिभिः । रथं सूतं ध्वजं वाहान्धनुर्वर्मशरादि यत् ॥ ६६ ॥
english_devnagari: dharmajñānaviraktyṛddhi tapovidyākriyādibhiḥrathaṃ sūtaṃ dhvajaṃ vāhāndhanurvarmaśarādi yat
translation: By means of His powers like righteousness, wisdom, renunciation, affluence, asceticism, knowledge, action and others, (He created for god Śiva) a chariot, a charioteer, the flag, horses, a bow, an armour, shafts and others.

devanagari: सन्नद्धो रथमास्थाय शरं धनुरुपाददे । शरं धनुषि सन्धाय मुहूर्तेऽभिजितीश्वरः ॥ ६७ ॥
english_devnagari: sannaddho rathamāsthāya śaraṃ dhanurupādadeśaraṃ dhanuṣi sandhāya muhūrte'bhijitīśvaraḥ
translation: Clad in the armour and seated in the chariot, god Śiva took up his bow and arrow. At noon, at the auspicious time, called Abhijit, Hara (Śiva) fixed the shaft to the bow and burnt down the three impregnable cities. Kettledrums were sounded in the sky which was crowded with hundreds of celestial cars.

devanagari: ददाह तेन दुर्भेद्या हरोऽथ त्रिपुरो नृप । दिवि दुन्दुभयो नेदुर्विमानशतसङ्कुलाः ॥ ६८ ॥
english_devnagari: dadāha tena durbhedyā haro'tha tripuro nṛpadivi dundubhayo nedurvimānaśatasaṅkulāḥ
translation: Clad in the armour and seated in the chariot, god Śiva took up his bow and arrow. At noon, at the auspicious time, called Abhijit, Hara (Śiva) fixed the shaft to the bow and burnt down the three impregnable cities. Kettledrums were sounded in the sky which was crowded with hundreds of celestial cars.

devanagari: देवर्षिपितृसिद्धेशा जयेति कुसुमोत्करैः । अवाकिरन्जगुर्हृष्टा ननृतुश्चाप्सरोगणाः ॥ ६९ ॥
english_devnagari: devarṣipitṛsiddheśā jayeti kusumotkaraiḥavākiranjagurhṛṣṭā nanṛtuścāpsarogaṇāḥ
translation: Gods, sages, manes (pitṛs) and leaders of Siddhas shouted “Victory” in joyous exclamation, and poured showers of flowers. The troupes of celestial nymphs danced in ecstasy.

devanagari: एवं दग्ध्वा पुरस्तिस्रो भगवान्पुरहा नृप । ब्रह्मादिभिः स्तूयमानः स्वं धाम प्रत्यपद्यत ॥ ७० ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ dagdhvā purastisro bhagavānpurahā nṛpabrahmādibhiḥ stūyamānaḥ svaṃ dhāma pratyapadyata
translation: Having thus burnt down the three (flying) cities, the venerable god Śiva (destroyer of cities) returned to his own residence while being praised by Brahmā and others.

devanagari: एवं विधान्यस्य हरेः स्वमायया विडम्बमानस्य नृलोकमात्मनः । वीर्याणि गीतान्यृषिभिर्जगद्गुरोर्लोकं पुनानान्यपरं वदामि किम् ॥ ७१ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ vidhānyasya hareḥ svamāyayā viḍambamānasya nṛlokamātmanaḥvīryāṇi gītānyṛṣibhirjagadgurorlokaṃ punānānyaparaṃ vadāmi kim
translation:
Such and others are the exploits of this Hari who with his Māyā Potency imitates the human form (i.e. way of life) that the heroic achievements of the Preceptor (or father) of the world which purify the worlds are sung by sages. What else should I relate next?
[back to top]
